title: AgenticSTS shows structured memory can beat ever-growing chat logs
friendlyTitle: Better memory helped an AI agent play a long game
summary: Researchers behind AgenticSTS replaced a growing agent transcript with five organized memory layers for the game Slay the Spire 2. The approach kept prompts around 5,000 tokens, won 6 of 10 low-difficulty runs, and used far fewer tokens than public agents that kept resending the whole game history.
friendlySummary: A research agent did not keep its whole chat history. Instead, it stored information in organized memory sections, which made it faster and cheaper to use.
whyItMatters: Long-running agents need memory systems that decide what to keep, retrieve, and discard. The result is a practical reminder that better agent architecture can cut cost and latency even when the underlying model stays the same.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This matters for work agents too. If an AI assistant is helping for hours, it needs smart memory instead of carrying every old message forever.
